key_insight: A symmetry-adapted single-orbital tight-binding model can accurately reproduce the low-energy band structure and spin splitting of chiral 1D hybrid lead-halide perovskites.
engineering_relevance: This provides a compact, interpretable basis for predicting and tuning band-edge, spin, optical, and transport behavior in chiral perovskite devices.
method: Builds an effective Hamiltonian with one orbital on each of four symmetry-related sites per unit cell.
Includes layer, in-plane sublattice, and spin degrees of freedom with symmetry-adapted spin-dependent hopping terms.
Uses separate parameter sets for conduction and valence bands and analyzes degeneracies via screw eigenvalues and antiunitary symmetry operators.
evidence_signal: The model reproduces DFT band dispersions and quantitatively captures spin splittings and leading spin-polarization patterns near the band edges.
watch_item: Because the model is fitted to low-energy physics with limited term content, its predictions may not generalize well away from the band edges or to symmetry-breaking perturbations.
paper_title: Single-orbital tight-binding model for chiral one-dimensional hybrid organic-inorganic lead halide perovskites
